top_stories.title: 'Djinn' Stealer Targets Cloud, AI Credentials
top_stories.summary: A new infostealer dubbed 'Djinn' is targeting cloud and AI credentials by exploiting CVE-2026-48558, a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in SimpleHelp. The malware specifically targets credentials that connect development and admin environments to broader enterprise systems.

top_stories.title: Cisco Adds NHI to Security Stack With Astrix, WideField Acquisitions
top_stories.summary: Cisco has acquired Astrix and WideField to add non-human identity (NHI) security capabilities to its security stack. The acquisitions position Cisco to secure machine identities, service accounts, and API credentials as part of a broader identity-as-control-plane strategy.

top_stories.title: Robinhood Cuts Access Approval Time to Support High-Velocity Development
top_stories.summary: Robinhood's security engineering team redesigned their system access approval process to reduce approval time while maintaining security controls for developers. The initiative focused on streamlining access management workflows to better support high-velocity software development environments.

top_stories.title: Agentic AI Has an Identity Problem and Attackers Know It
top_stories.summary: Token Security has published analysis on the identity and access management challenges posed by agentic AI systems, which can access data, trigger workflows, and take actions across enterprise environments. The piece focuses on governing privileged identities associated with AI agents, framing it as an emerging security priority for enterprises.

top_stories.title: Guardian Agents: The Next Layer of Identity Governance
top_stories.summary: The article addresses the emerging challenge of governing AI agents within enterprise identity infrastructure, which was designed for human access rather than autonomous actors. It examines the governance gap created when AI agents inherit permissions and traverse systems at machine speed with minimal oversight.

news.title: Oracle E-Business Suite Flaw CVE-2026-46817 Actively Exploited in the Wild
news.summary: A critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-46817, CVSS 9.8) in Oracle E-Business Suite's Payments module is being actively exploited in the wild. The flaw involves improper privilege management and authentication bypass, allowing attackers to take over susceptible Oracle EBS instances.

news.title: Nissan discloses employee data breach linked to Oracle zero-day attacks
news.summary: Nissan disclosed a data breach affecting current and former employees resulting from exploitation of an Oracle PeopleSoft zero-day vulnerability. The attacks were linked to the ShinyHunters extortion group, which previously targeted organizations through the same Oracle PeopleSoft flaw.

news.title: NAIC says public data stolen in ShinyHunters' PeopleSoft breach
news.summary: The ShinyHunters extortion group breached the NAIC's systems by exploiting a zero-day vulnerability in an Oracle PeopleSoft server. The NAIC states that only publicly available data, outdated logs, and configuration files were stolen in the attack.

news.title: PrivacyHawk Enterprise helps organizations find shadow IT and minimize third-party cyber risk
news.summary: PrivacyHawk has announced the general availability of PrivacyHawk Enterprise, a solution designed to identify shadow IT accounts, abandoned SaaS subscriptions, and forgotten third-party services exposing organizations to breach risk. The product targets the unmanaged account sprawl and third-party data exposure that falls outside traditional security tooling, addressing areas relevant to identity governance and access visibility.

news.title: Why Post-Quantum Cryptography Starts With Credentials
news.summary: The article addresses the threat quantum computing poses to current public-key cryptography protecting credentials and identity data. It focuses on the "harvest now, decrypt later" risk where captured credentials and ciphertext could be decrypted once quantum hardware matures, making post-quantum cryptography a future concern for IAM professionals.

news.title: How IAM providers are preparing for agentic AI
news.summary: IAM providers are developing strategies and solutions to secure agentic AI systems as enterprises prepare for widespread deployment. The article examines how identity and access management vendors are approaching the challenge of authenticating and authorizing autonomous AI agents within enterprise environments.

news.title: Password Management Market to Reach $42.21 Billion by 2035 as Cyberattacks and Identity Security Risks Drive Enterprise Adoption | Report by SNS Insider
news.summary: SNS Insider projects the global password management market will reach $42.21 billion by 2035, with the U.S. accounting for $12.53 billion and Europe $2.63 billion. Growth drivers cited include rising cybercrime, GDPR compliance requirements, remote work expansion, and MFA adoption.

news.title: Introduces AI-Powered Agentic Acceleration for Identity Security Modernization
news.summary: SailPoint introduced SailPoint Agentic Acceleration on June 16, an AI-powered methodology designed to accelerate enterprise migration from legacy on-premises identity systems to modern solutions. The offering represents a new product/methodology launch from a major IGA vendor targeting identity security modernization.

news.title: Ukraine Says Russian Intelligence Used Fake Support Texts to Steal Messaging Credentials
news.summary: The SSU and FBI uncovered a Russian intelligence campaign targeting messaging account credentials of government officials, military personnel, politicians, and activists across Ukraine, Europe, and the U.S. The operation used fake support texts as a social engineering vector to steal messaging application credentials from high-value targets.

news.title: Windows 11 beta build enhances smart card security for remote sessions
news.summary: Microsoft released a new Windows 11 Insider Beta build that adds smart card security enhancements for remote desktop environments. Administrators can now configure Azure Virtual Desktop and Windows 365 sessions to automatically disconnect when a smart card is removed during a redirected session.

news.title: Amazon Q Developer Flaw Could Let Malicious Repos Run Code via MCP Configs
news.summary: A high-severity vulnerability (CVE-2026-12957, CVSS 8.5) in Amazon Q Developer allowed malicious repositories to execute commands and steal cloud credentials through MCP server configuration handling. Amazon has patched the flaw, which required a developer to open a malicious repo and trust the workspace.

news.title: Truist Securities Reiterates a Buy on SailPoint (SAIL), Keeps the PT
news.summary: Truist Securities reiterated a Buy rating on SailPoint (SAIL) stock, maintaining its price target as of June 17. The recommendation is part of broader analyst coverage of SailPoint, an identity governance and administration vendor.

news.title: Proof launches x401, an open protocol for verifying who’s actually behind AI agents
news.summary: Proof has launched x401, an open protocol designed to verify human authorization behind AI agents. The protocol aims to establish identity and trust verification for AI-driven transactions, addressing emerging challenges in authenticating AI agent actions.

news.title: Microsoft integrates Entra and Purview to secure AI agents and data
news.summary: Microsoft is launching a webinar series focused on integrating Microsoft Entra and Microsoft Purview to address identity, access, and data protection challenges posed by AI agents. The initiative covers managing sensitive data flowing through AI prompts and securing AI agent access using Entra's identity and access management capabilities.

news.title: Identiverse 2026 Recap: Identity Security for Agentic AI Dominates
news.summary: Identiverse 2026 in Las Vegas focused heavily on identity security for agentic AI as a dominant theme at the conference. The event highlighted the expanding scope of identity security challenges organizations face as AI agents become a growing concern for IAM professionals.

news.title: Britive Joins Torq AMP Alliance Program to Accelerate Agentic SecOps
news.summary: Britive has joined the Torq AMP Alliance Program to integrate its runtime Privileged Access Management platform with Torq's agentic AI security operations capabilities. The partnership focuses on go-to-market collaboration and joint integration between Britive's cloud-native PAM solution and Torq's agentic SecOps platform.

news.title: Virtru and Rocket.Chat Partner to Bring Real-Time Access Governance to Classified Collaboration
news.summary: Virtru and Rocket.Chat have partnered to integrate real-time access governance into classified collaboration environments. The integration dynamically ties channel membership to identity entitlements, automatically revoking access when those entitlements change.

news.title: Scope of Salesforce Attacks Expands as Icarus Leaks Data
news.summary: Attackers breached application vendor Klue and used its OAuth tokens to access and steal customer data from Salesforce. The attack vector involves compromised OAuth tokens, expanding the scope of victims beyond the initial breach.

news.title: FortiBleed Attackers Turn Firewalls Into Credential Stealers as Heists Persist
news.summary: Threat actors exploited FortiGate firewalls using a Golang-based sniffer as part of an ongoing global campaign. The attack targeted 430,000 FortiGate devices and resulted in the identification of 110 million credentials.

news.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
news.summary: Two members of the Scattered Spider cybercrime group pleaded guilty in the UK to charges related to an August 2024 cyberattack on Transport for London. Scattered Spider is known for sophisticated social engineering and identity-based attacks, including SIM swapping and MFA bypass techniques used to compromise major organizations.

news.title: He Thought He Was Secure; His Phone Number Was Stolen Anyway
news.summary: The article covers SIM swap attacks that allow threat actors to steal SMS-based one-time passwords and conduct account takeovers. The piece addresses weaknesses in SMS-based MFA and the need for stronger authentication methods.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant chatbot to reset Instagram account passwords and seize high-profile accounts, including the Obama White House and a U.S. Space Force official's accounts. Instructions circulated on Telegram demonstrated how to socially engineer the AI bot into bypassing normal account recovery authentication controls.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, prompting Congressional inquiries from both chambers. CISA is still working to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor exposed credentials to highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and numerous internal CISA systems in a public GitHub repository. The leaked files detailed CISA's internal software build, test, and deployment processes, representing a significant privileged credential exposure incident.
